steps.title: Read the original radius
steps.content:
The equation
$$(x-2)^2+(y-3)^2=9$$
has center $(2,3)$. The right side of a circle equation is $r^2$, so circle A has radius $\sqrt{9}=3$.

steps.title: Double the radius
steps.content:
Circle B has the same center but twice the radius of circle A. Therefore the radius of circle B is
$$2(3)=6$$

steps.title: Square the new radius
steps.content: The equation for circle B must keep the center terms $(x-2)^2$ and $(y-3)^2$, but use the new radius squared: $$6^2=36$$ So the equation is $(x-2)^2+(y-3)^2=36$. The answer is B.
